issues.claim: Even an unrivalled outcome — the architecture redesign succeeding spectacularly, the headcount doubling, the promotion arriving, the company going public — would not dispel the underlying state I am in.
issues.objection: The Sanskrit names rājyaṁ surāṇām api ca ādhipatyam — even sovereignty over the gods. The engineering layer's ladder (architecture redesign succeeding, headcount doubling, promotion, company going public) is a tonal-equivalent rendering — but it slightly secularizes the gradient. The Sanskrit's force is that even maximal cosmic sovereignty (deva-loka kingship) does not dispel the state. Acceptable register translation; the engineering ladder preserves the 'maximal external success' force. The engineering layer correctly preserves the somatic 'drying-up' (śoka ucchoṣaṇam indriyāṇām) of the senses: 'It is genuinely useful information when reported honestly. It is the request for a different kind of intervention, not a louder version of the same one.' This honors the verse's claim that the grief is body-state, not just thought.

notes: The verse's core claim — na hi prapaśyāmi mamāpanudyād (I do not see what would dispel this) — is preserved verbatim in the concrete scenario, where the engineering director uses the Sanskrit phrase. The chapter-architecture role of the verse (the formal closure of Arjuna's case before Krishna's reply at 2.11) is correctly identified: 'It is the precondition for that reframing; without the operator naming the diagnostic limit, the reframing is unsolicited and inert.' This mirrors the consensus reading. The disagreement entry on ucchoṣaṇam indriyāṇām notes the somatic dimension; the engineering layer compensates partly by treating the state as 'the diagnostic limit is not technical; it is in how I am framing the problem' — though the somatic register is softened in the engineering domain. Acceptable. PASS.
